modules.name: Calendar
modules.description: Main calendar view for scheduling and viewing meetings

modules.name: MeetingsList
modules.description: List view of all meetings with filters

modules.name: ActionItems
modules.description: Track and manage action items from all meetings

modules.name: MeetingDetail
modules.description: Full meeting detail with notes, action items, attendees

modules.name: AvailabilitySettings
modules.description: Configure personal availability and working hours

events.name: meeting:created
events.description: Notify attendees of new meeting invite

events.name: meeting:rsvp_updated
events.description: Broadcast RSVP changes to organizer and attendees

events.name: meeting:notes_updated
events.description: Real-time notes sync for concurrent editors

events.name: action_item:created
events.description: Notify assignee of new action item

flows.name: Schedule Meeting
flows.trigger: Organizer fills meeting form and clicks Save
steps: Check attendee availability via free/busy API
Warn organizer of conflicts but allow override
Create meeting record with status = scheduled
Create MeetingAttendee rows for each attendee
Send calendar invites via email with ICS attachment
Broadcast meeting:created to attendees via websocket
If recurring → generate meetings for next 90 days, schedule job to generate more

flows.name: RSVP to Meeting
steps: If all required attendees accepted → set meeting status = confirmed

flows.name: Complete Meeting
steps: Prompt to add meeting notes and action items
Send post-meeting summary email with notes + action items
Mark action items with due dates as tracked

computed_fields.name: open_action_items
computed_fields.formula: COUNT(meeting_action_items) WHERE status IN ('open','in_progress') AND meeting_id = meetings.id

domain_rules: Meeting end time must be after start time
Recurring meeting instances store individual meeting records linked via recurring_series_id
Cancelling a recurring meeting: options are 'this only', 'this and following', 'all'
RSVP status defaults to pending; organizer automatically accepted
Availability check is advisory only; organizer can override conflicts
Meeting notes are editable by all attendees until 24 hours after meeting ends
Action items created in notes editor are auto-parsed and saved as MeetingActionItem rows
External guests invited via email with unique token-based RSVP links
generation_rules: Use FullCalendar or custom calendar grid for the CalendarView; do not use a date picker library
ICS file generation for calendar invite emails using ical-generator npm package
Recurring series uses rrule npm package for RRULE parsing and occurrence generation
Availability check overlays all attendees' calendars including external calendar sync
Meeting notes use collaborative rich text (TipTap or similar) with websocket sync
Action item extraction: detect lines starting with [] or AI-identify tasks in notes
Free/busy query aggregates: org meetings + external calendar events (if synced)
All times stored in UTC; timezone field for display conversion in UI

org_id_injection.middleware: req.body.org_id is stripped; injected from req.user.org_id

conflict_response.message: Record was modified by another user. Refresh and retry.

concurrency.high_contention_tables: see entities with high write concurrency

audit_trail.triggers: application-level (before write hook) — never DB trigger to avoid log bloat

standard_constraints.name: chk_{table}_deleted_order
standard_constraints.expression: deleted_at IS NULL OR deleted_at >= created_at

db_constraints_policy.enum_validation: DB ENUM types preferred over VARCHAR + CHECK; enum values defined in global enums registry

soft_delete_policy.filter: deleted_at IS NULL applied globally via query builder middleware
soft_delete_policy.cascade: Parent delete soft-deletes children via application cascade, not DB cascade
soft_delete_policy.hard_delete: Prohibited in production; available only via admin CLI with audit log
soft_delete_policy.restore: Set deleted_at = NULL; triggers audit log entry

naming_conventions.date_fields: Always use _at suffix for TIMESTAMPTZ, _date suffix for DATE
naming_conventions.id_fields: Primary key is always id; foreign keys are {entity}_id
